samples.target_compile_errors:
error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/simple_asgn_3/sample_001/target.rs:1:14
  |
1 | fn main() -> i32 {
  |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
  |
  = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`

warning: variable `p` is assigned to, but never used
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/simple_asgn_3/sample_001/target.rs:3:13
  |
3 |     let mut p: Option<&char> = None;
  |             ^
  |
  = note: consider using `_p` instead
  = note: `#[warn(unused_variables)]` (part of `#[warn(unused)]`) on by default

warning: value assigned to `p` is never read
 --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/expressions/simple_asgn_3/sample_001/target.rs:5:5
  |
5 |     p = Some(&c);
  |     ^
  |
  = help: maybe it is overwritten before being read?
  = note: `#[warn(unused_assignments)]` (part of `#[warn(unused)]`) on by default

error: aborting due to 1 previous error; 2 warnings emitted

For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0277`.
